name: A.J. Brown
headline: Bona fide alpha lands a fresh featured role in New England
body:
Even in a frustrating 2025, Brown still bent defenses toward him: a 26.1% target share, 0.614 WOPR and 31.8% of Philadelphia's air yards, with a positive 0.278 EPA per play across 121 looks. The dip to 1,003 yards and 142.3 fantasy points (WR18) was a volume and efficiency correction off his 0.637 EPA monster 2024, not a sign of decline at 28. His hands stayed elite, just one drop on 79 catchable balls, and he buried 14 catches of 20-plus yards.

Now he leaves a crowded Eagles pecking order to become the undisputed No. 1 for an ascending Drake Maye in a pass-leaning attack. That setup points squarely at a target share north of his recent marks and the bounce-back projection of roughly 90 grabs, 1,273 yards and eight scores. The schedule grades tough, but volume this concentrated is exactly how WR1 ceilings get rebuilt. Draft him as a back-end WR1.
